idea.hook: What's the UNIVERSE'S MOST POWERFUL MAGNET? 🧲🤯
facts: Typical Neutron Star (Pulsar): Up to 10^12 Gauss.
MAGNETAR: Up to 10^15 Gauss (1 Quadrillion Gauss!)
idea.payoff: A Magnetar's field is so strong it would permanently wipe ALL your credit cards from 600 miles away... and strip the electrons from your atoms!
idea.title: Universe's Strongest Magnet?! 🧲🤯

scenes.text: What's the UNIVERSE'S MOST POWERFUL MAGNET? 🧲🤯
scenes.visual: A dark, deep space background, slowly swirling with faint distant stars. A subtle, mysterious glow emanates from the center.

scenes.text:
Your Refrigerator Magnet: ~50 Gauss.
Earth's Magnetic Field: ~0.5 Gauss.
scenes.visual: Left: A simple icon of a refrigerator magnet. Right: A stylized Earth globe with faint, swirling magnetic field lines around it.

scenes.text:
Powerful MRI Machine: ~3 Tesla (30,000 Gauss).
Typical Neutron Star (Pulsar): Up to 10^12 Gauss.
scenes.visual: Left: An icon of an MRI machine with a subtle, contained magnetic field effect. Right: An artistic depiction of a pulsar, with bright, rotating beams of light emerging from its poles.

scenes.text: MAGNETAR: Up to 10^15 Gauss (1 Quadrillion Gauss!)
scenes.visual: A highly intense, swirling, and distorted visualization of a magnetar's magnetic field. Energy arcs crackle across the screen. The magnetar itself is a tiny, super-dense star at the core.

scenes.text: A Magnetar's field would permanently wipe ALL your credit cards from 600 miles away... and strip the electrons from your atoms!
scenes.visual: The screen becomes a chaotic, abstract representation of atomic particles being torn apart. Faint, ghost-like credit card icons appear and rapidly disintegrate into streaks of light, dissolving into the intense magnetic field effect.
